title: Cued Recall of Paired Associates with Open-Loop Stimulation at Encoding or Retrieval
description: This dataset contains intracranial electrophysiological recordings and behavioral data from a paired associates memory task with open-loop electrical brain stimulation. Participants studied word pairs, completed a distractor task, and performed cued recall while receiving stimulation at either encoding or retrieval phases. The study involved clinical collaboration across multiple sites and provides detailed stimulation parameters including electrode locations, amplitude, frequency, and pulse characteristics.
methods_description: Participants performed a paired associates learning task consisting of 25 lists per session with the structure: encoding (6 word pairs, 4000 ms each), distractor (arithmetic problems), and cued recall (5000 ms per response). Open-loop electrical stimulation was delivered to hippocampal and entorhinal cortex electrodes during encoding (3 of 6 pairs) or retrieval (3 of 6 cues) on 20 randomized lists. Stimulation parameters included amplitude, pulse frequency, pulse width, and pulse count. Intracranial recordings were acquired using Blackrock (250 nV units) or Medtronic (0.1 µV units) systems with monopolar and bipolar montages.
